codeReview.summary: The watchlist UI, store, and persistence are implemented cleanly, but the alerting path is wired to the filtered `activeNews` view instead of the full incoming dataset. That means counts and notifications are wrong whenever the user has an active region/search/severity/entity filter, which breaks the watch/alert contract.

issues.description: The watchlist effect calls `checkNewArticles(activeNews)`, but `activeNews` is already narrowed by the current UI filters. Users who are focused on another region or search term can miss new watched-region/topic/entity hits elsewhere because watch counts and notifications only reflect the filtered view, not the newly arrived dataset.
issues.suggestedFix: Drive watch matching from the unfiltered live/canonical event set and reserve `activeNews` for display-only filtering; then recompute notifications from that global dataset on refresh.

summary: Fail. The alert pipeline depends on filtered map state, so watch notifications can silently miss real matches outside the current view.
